message:
viz: migrate visualization data from embedded HTML to SQLite dataset

Complete the data pipeline migration by extracting classification data,
generating the SQLite database, and updating the HTML to load data via
sql.js WASM instead of parsing embedded JavaScript arrays.

spec_evolution_classification_v1.json (4,906 lines):
  - Extracted classification data mapping each spec section to its
    evolution phase (EARLY/MIDDLE/LATE) with section-level granularity
  - 137 commits classified across the full spec evolution history
  - Deterministic output with content hash for reproducibility

spec_evolution_v1.sqlite3 (2.5 MB):
  - Normalized SQLite database generated by generate-spec-evolution-sqlite.py
  - Contains 137 commits with full patch data, classification labels,
    and computed metrics (additions, deletions, impact scores)
  - Schema version 1 with integrity hashes for OPFS cache validation
  - Loaded by the visualization HTML via sql.js WASM at runtime,
    replacing the previous approach of inlining all data as JS literals

spec_evolution_v1.sqlite3.config.json:
  - Companion metadata with SHA-256 hashes of the classification input,
    source dataset, and generated database
  - Used by the visualization's OPFS caching layer to detect stale
    cached copies and trigger re-downloads only when data changes

visualization HTML (-5,281 / +501 lines, net -4,780):
  - Removed embedded CLASS_EARLY/MIDDLE/LATE arrays and inline commit
    data that previously bloated the HTML to ~23K lines
  - Added sql.js WASM integration: loads sql-wasm.js from jsDelivr CDN,
    fetches the SQLite database, and executes structured queries
  - Added OPFS (Origin Private File System) caching: the database is
    cached in the browser's persistent storage keyed by the config hash,
    avoiding re-downloads on subsequent visits
  - Data loading now happens via loadEvolutionDataset() which handles
    cache lookup, fetch fallback, and SQL query execution
  - The visualization's functional behavior is unchanged — same charts,
    same interactions, same Cloudflare Pages deployment target

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
